True North  

Description

1: True North

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 5 Skill: Divination
Casting Time: 2 Recast Time: 4
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 0
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: No Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Target Type: Self Spell Type: Beneficial
Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You spin to face north.

Game Description

Provides a magical sense of direction, changing your heading to point directly North.

Items with this effect

  • Bag of Chronobines
  • Bag of Doubloons
  • Bag of Ebon Crystals
  • Bag of Faycitum
  • Bag of Orux
  • Bag of Phosphenes
  • Bag of Phosphites
  • Bag of Platinum
  • Bag of Radiant Crystals
  • Brass Clockwork Abacus
  • Bronze Clockwork Abacus
  • Bundle of Defiant Armor
  • Dagger of Falsehood
  • Frightening Writ
  • Gold Clockwork Abacus
  • Golden Insignia of Quarm Event I
  • Igneous Crystalline Ember

Post Comment
Multiple items miscategorized as having this spell effect
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2014 at 8:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1684714731626
6 posts
I ended up on this page after looking at the item: "Package of Defiant Armor" only to discover that several items are listed as having the effect of spell ID #205 which is the spell "true north", originally.

Edited, Sep 19th 2014 10:31am by kuzetsa
▲ Page top
Re: Multiple items miscategorized as having this spell effec
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2014 at 9:34 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1691367413219
Guru
2,689 posts
They just lump all gift and such effects as an alias under this one. All of the aliased versions do something special and don't actually point you to true north. It's an easier way of doing special need effects that won't get used again, so they don't have to waste a development time on an effect specific for the special need. At least that's my assumption.

There are others besides True North that are aliased alot.

Yther Ore.
▲ Page top
true north
QuoteReply
# Oct 09 2009 at 5:13 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592274645684
77 posts
once a long time ago, when people made their own maps in game it was a useful spell to have, but at that lvl i woulg get it for a cheap mana cost to level up your divination.

asd
QuoteReply
# May 31 2008 at 6:09 AM Rating: Decent

we was using this when we have senseheading skill :D

# Jan 15 2007 at 11:35 AM Rating: Decent

Scholar
129 posts
what the point of this spell? lol dont ever1 have a compass?
▲ Page top
hmmm
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2008 at 3:44 PM Rating: Decent
Ebelch
View User Forum
626 posts
This is a great spell to practice your Divination skill.
▲ Page top
hmmm
QuoteReply
# Nov 11 2007 at 2:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592311204863
9 posts
Once upon a time there was no compass. Sense heading also started at 0 not 200, and had to be built. If you succeeded on sense heading it would tell you which direction you were facing. If you failed it did not. There also were not maps.

Back then this spell was useful. Now its useless.
▲ Page top
MAG only spell now
QuoteReply
# May 03 2006 at 10:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1686529156677
View User Forum
Scholar
119 posts
They have changed this spell to be only able to be used by Mages (level 1 spell)
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# May 04 2006 at 8:41 PM Rating: Decent
Kalysta
Scholar
1,087 posts
depends where you get the spell
some copies list by class merchants, since the spells were spread out between levels more.

neriak cleric guild, still lists
clr1 pal3 shm1 nec2 wiz1 mag1 enc1

And no changes to useable classes on lucy

▲ Page top
To all noobs
QuoteReply
# Mar 08 2006 at 9:24 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591836613554
142 posts
EverQuest didn't always used to be a hand holder game. You didn't start with 200 Sense Heading, and you had no compass, nor did you have a map. True North was one of the most reliable ways to find where you were pointing at early levels.
▲ Page top
5
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2006 at 4:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592770491459
3 posts
But! isnt the game a while ago your compass could be wrong, one time mine just kept spinning....wierd
▲ Page top
using the true north spell
QuoteReply
# Dec 26 2005 at 1:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1602461961302
104 posts
i thought that this is a uselees dumb spell
But ,this is the very best way to raise your divinataion skills > its a lvl 1 spell,uses very little mana, and chain cast this spell and watch your skill go up,it helps when you get a higher lvl div. type spell they wont fizzzle as much
▲ Page top
trolls
QuoteReply
# May 08 2005 at 1:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592269528154
Scholar
71 posts
Gartok in Neriak Foreign Quarter sells this
▲ Page top
Awesome
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2005 at 5:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592261657747
34 posts
This spell is the best spell in the game, anytime im lost, i NEVER use my map, always resort to this spell, why use a map that tells your exact location? It makes more sense to get upset and frustrated than click some little clicky click thing to see your map.
▲ Page top
RE: Awesome
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2006 at 3:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591863751171
877 posts
The map is a relatively new feature to the game. True North has been around since long before everyone had an innate Sense Heading of 200. True North used to have a very real purpose. Now, its reduced to Divination practice.
What was Sony to do? Remove an oldie from the game? The noobs may not respect it, but this thing made my days in Everfrost easier before you were born.
▲ Page top
RE: Awesome
QuoteReply
# Mar 16 2005 at 1:09 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591821720998
Scholar
54 posts
Quote:
This spell is the best spell in the game, anytime im lost, i NEVER use my map, always resort to this spell, why use a map that tells your exact location? It makes more sense to get upset and frustrated than click some little clicky click thing to see your map.


I don't know why I'm dignifying this with a response... maybe 'cause it made me laugh. (Side effects of sleep deprivation. /sigh) I'm just overcome by the compulsion to say...
BACKSPACE

My apologies for an utterly useless post. >.<
▲ Page top
Helps to raise Div
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2004 at 3:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591812039183
38 posts
This spell is pretty much useless for telling direction now that we all have a compass. Nevertheless, I would recommend buying this spell. It is cheap to buy, uses little mana, and repops quickly, which helps a LOT when you need to work on your Divination skill.
Being an Enchanter, I need to use illusions for certain things. I used the hell outta this spell to get my Div skill up so I could use the better illusions.

<3 Belle

Edited, Wed Jul 28 16:50:03 2004
▲ Page top
Use?
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2004 at 8:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1623630129087
Scholar
404 posts
Does anyone know if this spell has any value since we now have north available all the time?
▲ Page top
RE: Use?
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2004 at 8:42 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1606090737819
Sage
159 posts
For my cleric, using True North was easy way to improve my "diviniation" skill. About the only use that I can think of for it now that our sense heading skill is pre set to 200.

258 posts
The merchant that sells them is the one with (General Spells) in the center. (he?)It stands next to Nonnie Texaker. She is in the mage/enchant/wizard guildhall in Ak'Anon.
